query: I want dessert but only have bananas, oats, and peanut butter.

queries: Here is an easy dessert recipe using only bananas, oats, and peanut butter:

Here is a 3-ingredient banana oat peanut butter dessert recipe:

recommendation.headline: You're literally three ingredients away from warm cookies — bananas, oats, and peanut butter are all you need tonight.
recommendation.why_it_fits: You've got exactly what these cookies are built on: ripe bananas for sweetness and binding, oats for body, and peanut butter to hold it all together. No flour, eggs, sugar, or oil required, so there's nothing to run out for. Make sure your bananas are nicely ripe and spotty — that's where all the sweetness comes from.

missing_items.note: Just a pinch sharpens the peanut butter flavor; skip it if your peanut butter is already salted.
Purely optional for a more dessert-y feel — the cookies are plenty good without them.

query: I have chicken thighs but no onions or garlic.

queries: Here is a delicious chicken thighs recipe seasoned with herbs and spices:
Here is an easy baked chicken thighs recipe using pantry staples:
Here is a flavorful chicken thighs recipe with lemon and paprika:

recommendation.headline: Sear-and-bake lemon-herb chicken thighs are exactly the move tonight — no onion or garlic required.
recommendation.why_it_fits: You've got the star ingredient, and the Lemon & Herb Roasted Chicken Thighs on Minimalist Baker leans entirely on dried herbs and lemon rather than fresh aromatics. The only garlic it mentions is an optional pinch of powder you can skip outright, so this fits your pantry cleanly. It's a simple sear-then-oven method that rewards a crispy skin with bright flavor.

missing_items.note: Core of the flavor; a splash of bottled lemon juice or a little white wine vinegar works in a pinch.
Any mix of dried oregano, thyme, or basil you have on hand covers this.
Purely a finishing touch — skip it and lean on the dried herbs if you don't have fresh.

primary_sources.title: Lemon & Herb Roasted Chicken Thighs - Minimalist Baker Recipes

recommendation.how_to_use_sources: Follow the Lemon & Herb Roasted Chicken Thighs page on Minimalist Baker start to finish for the sear-and-bake timing and the herb-lemon seasoning; just leave out the optional garlic powder. If you want a warmer, more savory profile, borrow the herbs de Provence and paprika seasoning idea from the Simple French Herbs Roast Chicken Thighs page on Cooking With Our CSA.

query: Vegan and gluten free dinner with lentils.

queries: Here is a delicious vegan and gluten-free dinner recipe made with lentils:
This hearty vegan gluten-free lentil dinner recipe is perfect for weeknights:
Try this flavorful plant-based lentil dinner that is naturally gluten-free:

recommendation.dish_name: Red Lentil Curry with Cilantro Chutney
recommendation.headline: Tonight, make the bright, 30-minute Red Lentil Curry from A Couple Cooks — naturally vegan, gluten-free, and built around the lentils you've got.
recommendation.why_it_fits: You wanted a vegan, gluten-free dinner starring lentils, and this red lentil curry checks every box without any swaps. It comes together in about half an hour from mostly pantry staples, and the fresh cilantro chutney gives it a lift that most weeknight lentil dishes miss. If you'd rather keep things simpler, a classic dahl is right there too.

missing_items.note: The base of the dish — if you only have brown or green lentils, cook them longer as they won't break down as softly.
Gives the curry its creaminess; full-fat is best but light works in a pinch.
Check the label to confirm it's gluten-free; most Thai brands are.
Needed for the chutney — skip the chutney and just squeeze lime if you don't have it.
Adds a sweet pop; leave them out or use a pinch of sugar if you prefer.
For serving — any gluten-free grain or naan alternative works.

primary_sources.title: Red Lentil Curry – A Couple Cooks
primary_sources.dish_name: Red Lentil Curry with Cilantro Chutney

recommendation.how_to_use_sources: Follow the Red Lentil Curry page at A Couple Cooks for the full method, timing, and the cilantro chutney — it's self-contained and clearly labeled vegan and gluten-free. If you want more vegetables in the pot, borrow the carrot, bell pepper, and spinach additions from the Red Lentil Dahl on Eat With Clarity and stir them in as the lentils simmer.
